action.hypothesis: The routed family did not get a fair read because its initial support selection was too close to baseline top-k and tripped the identical-to-baseline guard. Making routed support depend on an independently initialized router with a direct, non-monotone routing bonus should produce a genuine architecture signal in proxy and reveal whether separate support selection can outperform the stalled gated search.
action.summary: Fix the routed prototype so its initial routing behavior is observably different from baseline top-k, then rerun routed in proxy at K=128 under the current strongest training regime.
action.self_review: This is a coherent architecture follow-up under severe stagnation: it repairs a blocked fundamentally different family instead of spending another round on minor parameter tuning. The main risk is that routed still underperforms on quality, but the previous crash was not informative, so this code-fix probe is higher value than more local gated adjustments.
notes_to_memory: Adjusted routed initialization so the router is independently initialized instead of near-copying the magnitude encoder, avoiding baseline-equivalent startup behavior.
Changed routed support scoring to include a direct router bonus, making selection meaningfully different from plain top-k while still decoding with gated magnitudes.
Local sanity checks now show routed top-k indices and top activations differ from baseline at initialization, and forward/backward both succeed.
next_hypotheses: If routed now runs cleanly and beats gated proxy materially, promote it and begin routed-specific stabilization as the main architecture path.
If routed runs but remains clearly worse, retire this version of the family and redirect architecture exploration elsewhere.
If routed quality is competitive but runtime is poor, keep the family alive and follow with a performance-focused code pass rather than discarding it on throughput alone.
